'''Christopher Melling''' (born 21 September 1984) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] player who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played in the [[Super League]] for the [[Wigan Warriors]] and [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins Rugby League]] (later renamed the [[London Broncos]]), as a {{rlp|fb|wg|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}, and for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]].

==Early life==
Melling was born in [[Ashton-in-Makerfield]], [[Greater Manchester, England]]. He was a pupil at [[Byrchall High School]]. 

Melling was a youth level international for [[England national rugby league team|England]], touring Australia and New Zealand with England Under 16s in 2001 and also representing England's U18 Academy.<ref name="Chris Melling">{{cite web|url=http://www.league.quins.co.uk/squad/index.php?player=81355&includeref=dynamic|title=Chris Melling|publisher=Quins|accessdate=2010-04-19}}{{dead link|date=January 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes}}</ref><ref name="Langley Leads GB to Victory">{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/bb_news_full.asp?newsid=430|title=Langley Leads GB to Victory|publisher=Bradford|date=2002-12-09|accessdate=2007-07-22|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930160132/http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/bb_news_full.asp?newsid=430|archive-date=30 September 2007|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Wigan Warriors===
Melling is a former Wigan Senior Academy captain who made several appearances for the Wigan first team.

In 2004's [[Super League IX]] it had seemed that Melling's career was in doubt following a serious knee injury suffered playing for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3522644.stm|title=Leeds 36-24 Wigan|publisher=BBC|date=2004-03-19|accessdate=2007-05-29}}</ref> Melling had been seen as a future star before having his season ended by a [[Anterior cruciate ligament injury|cruciate ligament injury]].<ref name="Wigan duo agree fresh deals">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/3599010.stm|title=Wigan duo agree fresh deals|publisher=BBC|date=2004-08-25|accessdate=2007-07-13}}</ref>

Upon his return to first team action in 2005's [[Super League X]] Chris made a further 10 first-grade appearances for the [[Wigan Warriors]] before picking up another [[knee injury]] requiring further [[surgery]].<ref name="Chris Melling 2">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/PlayerDisplayRL.ink?skip=11&season=2006/2007&Playertype=s|title=Chris Melling|publisher=Quins|date=2007-07-22|accessdate=2007-07-22|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224745/http://www.quins.co.uk/PlayerDisplayRL.ink?skip=11&season=2006%2F2007&Playertype=s|archivedate=30 September 2007|url-status=dead|df=dmy}}</ref>

Melling returned to full fitness in 2006, but found first team opportunities limited, due to the emergence of [[Chris Ashton]] at the club.<ref name="Melling ready to make Wigan pay again for mistake">{{cite news|url=http://sport.independent.co.uk/rugby_league/article2631116.ece|title=Melling ready to make Wigan pay again for mistake|publisher=Independent|date=2007-06-08|accessdate=2007-07-22|location=London|first=Dave|last=Hadfield|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930171029/http://sport.independent.co.uk/rugby_league/article2631116.ece|archive-date=30 September 2007|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Chris Melling moved to {{rlp|hk}} in Wigan's academy, impressing enough to be voted as the Under-21s Academy 'Player of the Year' for 2006.<ref name="League Playing squad 2006/2007">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/PlayerDisplayRL.ink?skip=11&season=2006/2007&Playertype=s|title=League Playing squad 2006/2007|publisher=Quins|date=2007-07-22|accessdate=2007-07-22|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224745/http://www.quins.co.uk/PlayerDisplayRL.ink?skip=11&season=2006%2F2007&Playertype=s|archivedate=30 September 2007|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

He left Wigan to pursue regular first team [[rugby league]].

===Harlequins RL===
[[File:Chris Melling Quins.JPG|thumb|left|300px|Melling in action for Harlequins RL]]
Melling was initially signed as a back-up {{rlp|hk}} to [[Chad Randall]],<ref name="Harlequins swoop for British trio">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/5369258.stm|title=Harlequins swoop for British trio|publisher=BBC|date=2006-09-21|accessdate=2007-06-13}}</ref> however the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international made the {{rlp|fb}} position his own. He has been compared to [[Wade McKinnon]] with the youngster being defensively solid and dependable, with an eye for a gap bringing the ball out in attack.<ref name="Sykes and Melling make the GB squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37449&newstype=z&matchid=&storytype=RL=|title=Sykes and Melling make the GB squad|publisher=Quins|date=2007-06-20|accessdate=2007-06-21|archive-date=30 September 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224446/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37449&newstype=z&matchid=&storytype=RL=|url-status=dead}}</ref> His move to [[London Broncos#Harlequins RL .282006.E2.80.932011.29|Harlequins]] was to have seen the youngster undergo a positional change with a move to hooker.<ref name="Quins land young British trio">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=32597&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL|title=Quins land young British trio|publisher=Quins RL|date=2006-09-21|accessdate=2007-06-13|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224530/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=32597&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL|archivedate=30 September 2007|df=dmy-all}}</ref> He had been previously employed throughout the backline, with most of his first team appearances coming as an outside back.

Melling impressed in 2007,<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/6757049.stm|title= Melling call-up a sign of the times|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-15|accessdate=2007-06-15| first=Phil| last=Gordos}}</ref> starting games at {{rlp|fb}} and throughout the backline and scoring four tries. In May 2007 Melling agreed to a new two-year deal with Harlequins Rugby League, midway through his first season at the [[Twickenham Stoop]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37376&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|title=New deal for Melling|publisher=Quins.co.uk|date=2007-05-29|accessdate=2007-05-29|archive-date=30 September 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224150/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37376&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|url-status=dead}}</ref> This will keep Melling in the [[London|capital]] until 2009.<ref>Lester, Joanna: "Joanna Lester chats to two stars who are particularly familiar with today's opposition…", game on!, Harlequins RL v Wigan Warriors, p41, 2 June 2007</ref> He is seen as the emerging star at the [[London Broncos#Harlequins RL .282006.E2.80.932011.29|Harlequins]], with Harlequins RL investing in young British talent.<ref name="Club-by-club guide: Harlequins">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/01/24/manual_160552.html|title=Club-by-club guide: Harlequins|publisher=Sportinglife|date=2007-07-22|accessdate=2007-07-22|archive-date=30 September 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930042724/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F07%2F01%2F24%2Fmanual_160552.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Representative football===
In June 2007 Melling was called up to the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] squad,<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/6744909.stm|title=Eight new faces in Lions squad|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-12|accessdate=2007-06-12}}</ref><ref name="Melling and Sykes named in GB squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37438&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|title=Melling and Sykes named in GB squad|publisher=Quins RL|date=2007-06-13|accessdate=2007-06-13|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224114/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37438&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|archivedate=30 September 2007|df=dmy}}</ref><ref name="What our GB player has to say">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37445&newstype=z&matchid=&storytype=RL|title=What our GB player has to say|publisher=Quins RL|date=2007-06-13|accessdate=2007-06-13|archive-date=28 September 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070928015910/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37445&newstype=z&matchid=&storytype=RL|url-status=dead}}</ref> and was {{rlp|fb}} in the [[test match (rugby league)|Test match]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref name="New-look GB select five new caps">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/6765035.stm|title= New-look GB select five new caps|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-20|accessdate=2007-06-20}}</ref> He was joined in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team#Current squad|Great Britain]] Test side by [[London Broncos#Harlequins RL .282006.E2.80.932011.29|Harlequins]] teammate [[Paul Sykes (rugby league footballer)|Paul Sykes]].<ref name="Melling and Sykes to play at Headingley">{{cite web|url=http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37475&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|title=Melling and Sykes to play at Headingley|publisher=Quins|date=2007-06-20|accessdate=2007-06-20|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930224312/http://www.quins.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=37475&newstype=n&matchid=&storytype=RL&Refid=|archivedate=30 September 2007|df=dmy}}</ref> Melling made his Great Britain début in the 42-14 victory over the [[France national rugby league team|French]] on 22 June 2007, despite carrying an injury.<ref name="Great Britain 42-14 France">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/6228054.stm|title= Great Britain 42-14 France|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-22|accessdate=2007-06-24}}</ref><ref name="Hungry Lions Please Smith">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/07/06/22/RUGBYL_Great_Britain_Quotes.html|title=Hungry Lions Please Smith|publisher=SportingLife|date=2007-06-22|accessdate=2007-06-24|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070930050258/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague%2F07%2F06%2F22%2FRUGBYL_Great_Britain_Quotes.html|archivedate=30 September 2007|df=dmy}}</ref><ref name="Morley finally gets on scoresheet as Smith's new-look turn up trumps">{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/jun/23/rugbyleague.sport|title=Morley finally gets on scoresheet as Smith's new-look turn up trumps|publisher=Guardian|date=2007-06-23|access-date=2007-07-21| location=London| first=Andy| last=Wilson}}</ref>

==Physiotherapy career==
Melling became a qualified [[physiotherapist]] after studying at the [[University of Central Lancashire]] in [[Preston, Lancashire|Preston]],<ref name="Wigan Player Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/SquadMember.asp?teamid=1&id=80|title=Wigan Player Profile|publisher=Wigan|date=2007-07-22|accessdate=2007-07-22|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927213459/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/SquadMember.asp?teamid=1&id=80|archivedate=27 September 2007|url-status=dead|df=dmy}}</ref> graduating in June 2006.<ref name="Lester, Joanna p41">Lester, Joanna: "Joanna Lester chats to full back and budding physiotherapist Chris Melling", game on!, Harlequins RL v Wakefield, p41, 14 April 2007</ref> He hoped to set up his own physiotherapy practise in the future.<ref name="Lester, Joanna p41"/>

After retiring as a player in 2013, Melling joined [[Warrington Wolves]] as a rehab specialist and academy physiotherapist,<ref>{{cite web |title=Warrington Wolves make youth appointments, Ben Lazenby is promoted and Gary Chambers returns |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/10824652.warrington-wolves-make-youth-appointments-ben-lazenby-is-promoted-and-gary-chambers-returns/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=14 April 2024 |date=21 November 2013}}</ref> and later worked as a physio at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Getting to know you, getting to know all about you |url=https://roughyeds.co.uk/2020/01/getting-to-know-you-getting-to-know-all-about-you-2/ |website=Oldham RLFC |access-date=14 April 2024 |date=22 January 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Halifax Panthers overhaul backroom staff with Finn and Grix appointments |url=https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/halifax-panthers-overhaul-backroom-staff-with-finn-and-grix-appointments-3453695 |website=Halifax Courier |access-date=14 April 2024 |date=11 November 2021}}</ref> Melling became head physio at [[Leigh Leopards]] in December 2022.
